none: A trio of people are seated around a table for breakfast.

body.creator: Tallulah Stallvik
body.value: In this shot, Reynolds is shot at the far end of the table with Alma and Cyril seen flanking him and looking much more prominent. This arrangement suggests that the two women are the ones actually in charge, a dynamic that will play out in the remainder of Phantom Thread.

body.creator: Tallulah Stallvik
body.value: The mise-en-scène here stresses the shift in the dynamic between Reynolds and the two most prominent women in his life. The two women in the foreground are shrouded in darkness while Reynolds is strongly backlit in the center of the frame. The lines of the table direct the eye towards him to produce the impression of a triangle in which Alma and Cyril are the mirrored legs. The women appear to be seated much further down the table, creating a sense of distance between them and Reynolds. The composition is largely symmetrical—but not quite. The doors appear to be slightly off center, creating the impression that a scene that should be balanced is unsettled.

body.creator: Tallulah Stallvik
body.value: Alma and Cyril’s behavior in this scene inverts the previous breakfast, as here Cyril is the one who disrupts Reynolds’s quiet time, though with unpleasant news rather than too much noise, and Alma is the one who remains quiet and inoffensive. Reynolds complains that he wishes he had been told this news later in the day, calling back to Cyril’s comment that it is important to his routine that breakfast be peaceful and quiet. It is clear that Alma has taken Cyril’s comments to heart, buttering her toast quietly and not speaking. Perhaps Cyril too has internalized Alma’s comment that Reynolds is “too fussy,” as she simply tells him to “buck up,” pushing back against his routine as Alma once did.
